acceptance_targets: [chapter_07_award_01] PASS if the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:223, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:459, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:507 leave Lena's doubling-down readable from action and image, with no more than one explicit sentence that explains what the embarrassment 'means.' FAIL if the revision still repeatedly tells the reader why Lena is reframing the day after the scene has already shown it.

[chapter_07_craft_01] PASS if the revised stretch anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:417, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:443, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:453 gives the reader a plausible physical or temporal window for Eli's ask while June is present, and June's dialogue no longer reads as if it is cueing the exact request in real time. FAIL if the ask still arrives in lockstep with June's in-room coaching without any visible mechanism that could support the later reveal.

[HIGH_CH07_DC_TRANSFER_MISMATCH] Pass if a reader can read snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3495-3525 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3543-3563 and state one exact dollar amount and one exact purpose for the D.C. rescue transfer. Fail if the chapter still reads like one version of an event the book later counts differently.

[MEDIUM_CH07_DEFAULT_FACIAL_TIC] Pass if a reader can feel June's recalibration in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3457-3466 through one concrete, non-generic action unique to this scene. Fail if the beat still relies on 'went still,' 'went flat,' 'went blank,' or a near-synonym as the main piece of characterization.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:223, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:459, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:507 leave Lena's doubling-down readable from action and image, with no more than one explicit sentence that explains what the embarrassment 'means.' FAIL if the revision still repeatedly tells the reader why Lena is reframing the day after the scene has already shown it.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps paraphrasing Lena's self-deception after the scene has already made it legible. Those interpretive sentences clarify the mechanism of humiliation-to-commitment too often, which reduces ambiguity and keeps the reader from doing enough inferential work for shortlist-level fiction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the explanatory commentary at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:223, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:459, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:507. Keep Lena's rationalization visible through choice, image, bodily reaction, and what she says yes to, but cut or sharpen the sentences that explicitly decode the meaning of the humiliation for the reader.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised stretch anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:417, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:443, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:453 gives the reader a plausible physical or temporal window for Eli's ask while June is present, and June's dialogue no longer reads as if it is cueing the exact request in real time. FAIL if the ask still arrives in lockstep with June's in-room coaching without any visible mechanism that could support the later reveal.
findings.problem: The concealed mechanics around the $280 ask are under-staged. June is physically in the room, actively guiding Lena through the decision, and the ask lands in near-perfect sync with that coaching. Given the continuity rule that major money asks require manual steering, the chapter needs a clearer on-page window or cover for how that escalation happens, or the later reveal risks feeling mechanically convenient instead of retrospectively inevitable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the sequence around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:417, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:435, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:443, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:453 so the money ask has a plausible concealed steering path. Options: give June a visible reason to step away or check a device, delay Eli's ask until she is no longer actively managing Lena in real time, or make June's advice less neatly pre-justify the exact transfer that follows.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can read snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3495-3525 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3543-3563 and state one exact dollar amount and one exact purpose for the D.C. rescue transfer. Fail if the chapter still reads like one version of an event the book later counts differently.
findings.problem: The D.C./Noelle salvage transfer is staged here as a $280 one-night bridge, but later archive material reassigns the late-September rescue money to different numbers and purposes. Since this humiliation scene is a structural hinge, the amount and reason for the transfer cannot drift later without damaging causality.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3495-3525, choose one canonical amount and one canonical purpose for this transfer. Then make the motel-room aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3543-3563 clearly correspond to that same payment rather than a fuzzier general rescue. If this chapter remains canon, later ledger chapters must be revised separately to match this figure; if not, revise the ask and send screen here now.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can feel June's recalibration in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3457-3466 through one concrete, non-generic action unique to this scene. Fail if the beat still relies on 'went still,' 'went flat,' 'went blank,' or a near-synonym as the main piece of characterization.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book pattern: the default facial/stasis shorthand 'went still/flat/blank' now appears in at least five chapters (1, 7, 12, 15, 16). This chapter should REPLACE its instance. The Wawa-cake aftermath is otherwise concrete and alive, so 'June went still for a beat' reads as a placeholder rather than a singular human reaction.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3457-3466, replace the 'went still' beat with a scene-specific physical tell tied to this exact aftermath, for example how June handles the Coke bottle, the crushed cake container, or the soaked dress hem. Keep the tonal shift from banter to seriousness, but do not let 'still/flat/blank' do the work here.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
